_note: Anna PV-focused russian_trap set — 4 items locking PV pair confusions and L1 calques specific to her family/daily-life register. Mirrors the look_for/look_after structure that was authored for Artem (rt_artem_b01-b02), plus pick_up vs take and turn_off vs close.

exercises.calque_trap: Russian 'искать' = search → English 'look FOR' (NOT 'look after'). The look_for / look_after pair shares base verb 'look' but particles split into opposite domains: 'for' = search, 'after' = take care of. Failure mode: blending into 'look after for X' or substituting 'look after' when meaning 'search'.
correct_answers: I'm looking for my phone
I am looking for my phone
I'm looking for the phone
I am looking for the phone

common_errors.pattern_label: look after (caregiving) instead of look for (search)
common_errors.feedback: ✗ 'Look after' = take care of (children, pets, the house). For 'искать' = search → 'look FOR'. ✓ 'looking FOR my phone'. The two PVs are a particle-swap trap: same base 'look', opposite meanings.
✗ 'Looking my phone' — 'look' as a search verb needs 'for'. ✓ 'looking FOR my phone'.

exercises.fallback_feedback: Target: 'I'm looking for my phone.' Russian 'искать' always maps to 'look FOR' — never 'look after', never bare 'look'.

exercises.calque_trap: Russian 'присматривать за' / 'смотреть за' = take care of → English 'look AFTER' (NOT 'look for'). Mirror of rt_anna_pv_b01: same base verb, opposite particle. Family scenario locks the contrast in the caregiving direction.
correct_answers: Who'll look after the kids in the evening
Who will look after the kids in the evening
Who's going to look after the kids in the evening
Who is going to look after the kids in the evening
Who'll look after the children in the evening
Who will look after the children in the evening

common_errors.pattern_label: look for (search) instead of look after (care)
common_errors.feedback: ✗ 'Look for the kids' = search for them (as if they're missing). For 'смотреть за' / 'присматривать за' = take care of → 'look AFTER'. ✓ 'look AFTER the kids'. Mirror of the искать/look-for trap: same base, opposite particle.

common_errors.pattern_label: watch (close but not the target PV)
common_errors.feedback: 'Watch the kids' is acceptable AmE conversational, but the standard PV for 'смотреть за / присматривать за' is 'look AFTER'. Practise the PV form.

exercises.fallback_feedback: Target: 'Who'll look after the kids in the evening?' Russian 'смотреть за / присматривать за' = 'look AFTER' — never 'look for' (that's search).

exercises.calque_trap: Russian 'забрать (откуда-то)' = collect/take from a place. The literal calque is 'take from X', but English idiom for collecting people, kids, packages, groceries from a location is 'pick up'. 'Take from school' sounds like removal/extraction; 'pick up from school' is the natural collection idiom.
correct_answers: Will you pick up Masha from kindergarten
Can you pick up Masha from kindergarten
Will you pick Masha up from kindergarten
Can you pick Masha up from kindergarten

common_errors.feedback: ✗ 'Take Masha from kindergarten' — direct calque of Russian 'забрать из'. The English idiom is ✓ 'pick up Masha' (separable: 'pick Masha up'). 'Pick up' carries the meaning of collection from a location; 'take from' implies removal.
'Get Masha from kindergarten' is understandable but not the natural choice. ✓ 'pick up Masha' is the standard PV for collecting kids from school/nursery.

exercises.fallback_feedback: Target: 'Will you pick up Masha from kindergarten?' Russian 'забрать (откуда-то)' = 'pick up' — the English collection idiom. 'Take from' is too literal.

exercises.calque_trap: Russian 'выключить' is sometimes calqued to English 'close' (closing a circuit metaphor — 'закрыть цепь'). Standard English for devices: 'turn off' or 'switch off'. 'Close the TV' / 'close the light' are recognisable Russian-L1 errors. 'Close' in English is for doors, windows, books, accounts — not devices.
correct_answers: Turn off the TV
Switch off the TV
Turn the TV off
Switch the TV off
Turn off the television
Switch off the television

common_errors.feedback: ✗ 'Close the TV' — Russian L1 calque (выключить ≈ closing a circuit). English doesn't say this. ✓ 'Turn off the TV' or 'switch off the TV'. 'Close' in English is for doors, windows, books, accounts — never devices.
✗ 'Off the TV' is incomplete — English needs the verb. ✓ 'Turn off' or 'switch off' the TV.

exercises.fallback_feedback: Target: 'Turn off the TV' or 'Switch off the TV.' Russian 'выключить' = turn off / switch off. Never 'close' for devices — that's a Russian-L1 calque.
